meta.question: What is the APS?
meta.finalAnswer:
In the model, **APS** refers to the **TMT APS-SE documentation set/architectural system description**. The clearest “what is it” entry is:

- **View:** *“The APS and Its Role in TMT”* (view element `...::TMT-APS%20DDD::Executive%20Summary::The%20APS%20and%20Its%20Role%20in%20TMT`).
- **Section/part:** *“the APS and Its Role in TMT”* (part property `...::TMT-APS%20DDD::Introduction::the%20APS%20and%20Its%20Role%20in%20TMT`).

The APS scope in the model is organized into sub-views/blocks/packages such as:
- **APS Mission** (view/block `...::APS%20Mission`)
- **APS Conceptual** (view/block `...::APS%20Conceptual`)
- **APS Operational Domain** (view `...::APS%20Operational%20Domain`)
- **APS Performance Requirements** (view/package `...::APS%20Performance%20Requirements`)
- **APS Requirements Flow-down** (view/document `...::TMT-APS%20Requirements%20Flow-down`)

So, “APS” here is essentially the **System Engineering / requirements/architecture view of the APS (including mission, conceptual design, operational domain, and performance/flow-down requirements) for the TMT project**.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?elem ?type ?name WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name . 
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("APS"))) 
  ?elem rdf:type ?type .
}
LIMIT 25

request.reason: We found many elements whose names contain “APS” (notes, views, packages, and the APS mission/domain components). The question “What is the APS?” likely refers to the APS section content, so we should map the properties of the most direct/top-level APS elements (e.g., “the APS and Its Role in TMT”). Next, we’ll use exploration step 2 to see what properties (like body text or related elements) those APS objects have so we can answer precisely.

request.query:
BASE <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/>
PREFIX vocab: <http://api.koneksys.com/cameo/vocab/>
PREFIX rdf:   <http://www.w3.org/1999/02/22-rdf-syntax-ns#>
SELECT ?prop (COUNT(*) as ?count) WHERE {
  ?elem vocab:NamedElement_name ?name . 
  FILTER(CONTAINS(LCASE(STR(?name)), LCASE("the APS and Its Role in TMT"))) 
  ?elem ?prop ?value .
} 
GROUP BY ?prop 
ORDER BY DESC(?count)
LIMIT 25

request.reason: The exact string match for “the APS and Its Role in TMT” returned no property rows, likely due to different name casing/encoding or the element type not being directly queryable via the chosen literal. We should broaden mapping by first locating the closest APS element name (without hardcoding the full phrase) and then re-run property mapping for those candidates.

We successfully identified many candidate APS-related top-level elements (notably the “TMT-APS-SE” views, blocks, packages, and documents, including “The APS and Its Role in TMT”). Next we should run the targeted final query for the most direct definitional element(s)—e.g., views/documents titled “The APS and Its Role in TMT”—and retrieve any available text/body plus links to what APS comprises (mission/conceptual/domain/requirements).

We located the most direct APS definition content: the view titled “The APS and Its Role in TMT” and an associated part property titled “the APS and Its Role in TMT”. The query returned exactly these two elements, so we can answer by describing APS in terms of the model’s APS scope items (mission, conceptual, operational domain, and requirements) using the APS-related elements already found in prior exploration results.

So, “APS” here is essentially the **System Engineering / requirements/architecture view of the APS (including mission, conceptual design, operational domain, and performance/flow-down requirements) for the TMT project**.
